title: voacanga bracteata var. zenkeri
intro: Voacanga bracteata var. zenkeri, also known as the African snuff or Voacanga bark, is a plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a stimulant and psychoactive agent, with effects similar to those of yohimbine. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as increased alertness, improved mood, and enhanced cognitive function, although its long-term effects and potential side effects are not well understood. Traditional herbal preparations of Voacanga bracteata var. zenkeri include powdered bark, snuff, and tinctures, which are often used in shamanic and traditional healing practices.
